skill.name: ml-pipeline-workflow
skill.description: Guides teams through end-to-end MLOps pipeline design, from data preparation and training to validation, deployment, and monitoring.
skill.summary: Plan production ML pipelines with reproducible stages, validation gates, deployment patterns, and monitoring practices.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as false positives. The file contains documentation, markdown code fences, and references to MLOps topics, with no executable shell commands, weak cryptographic implementation, network access, or prompt injection attempt found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged external command locations are markdown inline code, fenced examples, or documentation references. No shell execution API or runnable command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown syntax and illustrative snippets, not Ruby or shell execution. I found no executable file or command invocation pattern in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines discuss machine learning pipeline concepts and headings. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic algorithm implementation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain MLOps documentation terms and no crypto primitives. The static match is inconsistent with the visible file content.

content.value_statement: ML projects often fail when training, validation, deployment, and monitoring are handled separately. This skill helps teams design reproducible MLOps workflows with clear stages and controls.
actual_capabilities: Outlines end-to-end ML pipeline stages from ingestion to monitoring.
Recommends orchestration patterns for Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow, and Prefect.
Covers data validation, feature engineering, dataset versioning, and lineage.
Explains training, experiment tracking, model comparison, and validation gates.
Describes deployment strategies such as canary, blue-green, rollback, and shadow releases.
limitations: Does not include executable pipeline templates in the reviewed file.
Does not validate a live data platform or cloud environment.
Does not train, deploy, or monitor models by itself.
Requires user-provided project details for architecture choices.

use_cases.title: Design a New ML Workflow
use_cases.description: Plan stages, dependencies, validation gates, and deployment paths before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Standardize MLOps Practices
use_cases.description: Create shared guidance for reproducible training, data versioning, experiment tracking, and rollback planning.

use_cases.title: Review Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Check an existing ML pipeline for missing validation, monitoring, rollout, and failure-handling practices.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a first ML workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a simple ML pipeline for my project. Include data preparation, training, validation, deployment, and monitoring stages.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Orchestration Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow, and Prefect for this ML pipeline. Recommend one option based on my team and infrastructure.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my ML workflow and propose validation gates for data quality, model performance, regression checks, and deployment approval.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production rollout plan for this model pipeline. Include canary release, rollback triggers, monitoring metrics, and incident response steps.

output_examples.input: Design a pipeline for weekly churn model training.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow with data ingestion, validation, feature generation, scheduled training, baseline comparison, registry approval, batch deployment, and drift monitoring.

output_examples.input: Review our pipeline before production deployment.
output: Add explicit data quality checks before feature generation.
Require model comparison against a fixed baseline.
Define rollback triggers for latency, accuracy, and drift.

output_examples.input: Help choose an orchestration approach for Kubernetes.
output_examples.output: A recommendation that weighs Kubeflow, Dagster, and Airflow against team skill, deployment target, artifact tracking, and operational complexity.

best_practices: Define each pipeline stage as an independently testable unit.
Version data, code, configuration, models, and evaluation results together.
Use automated validation and rollback criteria before production traffic changes.
anti_patterns: Deploying models without baseline comparison or performance regression checks.
Mixing training logic, serving logic, and feature transformations without clear ownership.
Relying on manual approvals without documented metrics, thresholds, and rollback steps.

faq.question: Does this skill create a complete MLOps platform?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning guidance and workflow structure. Implementation still requires project-specific code and infrastructure.

faq.question: Which orchestration tools does it discuss?
faq.answer: It references Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow Pipelines, and Prefect as possible orchestration choices.

faq.question: Can it help with data preparation?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers validation, feature engineering, dataset versioning, lineage, and train-validation-test split planning.

faq.question: Does it include deployment strategies?
faq.answer: Yes. It discusses shadow deployment, canary release, blue-green deployment, rollback mechanisms, and production monitoring.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated ML systems?
faq.answer: It can support planning, but regulated systems need additional compliance, audit, privacy, and governance review.

faq.question: Does the reviewed skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed file is documentation and contains no executable command runner.
